children.heading: Eligible entity
children.label: 1 Eligible entity
children.text: The term “eligible entity” means a governmental agency or nonprofit organization.

children.heading: Emergency feeding organization
children.label: 2 Emergency feeding organization
children.text: The term “emergency feeding organization” has the meaning given the term in .section 7501 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the supplemental nutrition assistance program established under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ( et seq.); and7 U.S.C. 2011

children.label: B
children.text: the programs for nutrition assistance under section 19 of such Act ().7 U.S.C. 2028

children.label: A
children.text: a hospital;

children.label: B
children.text: a Federally-qualified health center (as defined in section 1905() of the Social Security Act (()));ll42 U.S.C. 1396d

children.label: C
children.text: a hospital or clinic operated by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs; or

children.label: D
children.text: a healthcare provider group.

children.label: i
children.text: benefits under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ( et seq.); or7 U.S.C. 2011

children.label: ii
children.text: medical assistance under a State plan or a waiver of such a plan under title XIX of the Social Security Act ( et seq.) and enrolled under such plan or waiver; and42 U.S.C. 1396

children.label: B
children.text: a member of a low-income household that suffers from, or is at risk of developing, a diet-related health condition.

children.heading: Food insecurity nutrition incentive grants
children.label: b Food insecurity nutrition incentive grants

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In each of the years specified in subsection (c), the Secretary shall make grants to eligible entities in accordance with paragraph (2).

children.label: i
children.text: an emergency feeding organization;

children.label: ii
children.text: an agricultural cooperative;

children.label: iii
children.text: a producer network or association;

children.label: iv
children.text: a community health organization;

children.label: v
children.text: a public benefit corporation;

children.label: vi
children.text: an economic development corporation;

children.label: vii
children.text: a farmers’ market;

children.label: viii
children.text: a community-supported agriculture program;

children.label: ix
children.text: a buying club;

children.label: x
children.text: a retail food store participating in the supplemental nutrition assistance program;

children.label: xi
children.text: a State, local, or tribal agency;

children.label: xii
children.text: another eligible entity that receives a grant under this subsection; and

children.label: xiii
children.text: any other entity the Secretary designates.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: C Federal share
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (D)(iii), the Federal share of the cost of carrying out an activity under this subsection shall not exceed 50 percent of the total cost of the activity.

children.label: I
children.text: in cash or in-kind contributions as determined by the Secretary, including facilities, equipment, or services; and

children.label: II
children.text: by a State or local government or a private source.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: In the case of a for-profit entity, the non-Federal share described in clause (i) shall not include services of an employee, including salaries paid or expenses covered by the employer.

children.heading: Tribal agencies
children.label: iii Tribal agencies
children.text: The Secretary may allow a Tribal agency to use funds provided to the Indian Tribe of the Tribal agency through a Federal agency (including the Indian Health Service) or other Federal benefit to satisfy all or part of the non-Federal share described in clause (i) if such use is otherwise consistent with the purpose of such funds.

children.label: i
children.text: meet the application criteria set forth by the Secretary; and

children.label: I
children.text: has the support of the State agency administering the supplemental nutrition assistance program;

children.label: II
children.text: would increase the purchase of fruits and vegetables by low-income households participating in the supplemental nutrition assistance program by providing an incentive for the purchase of fruits and vegetables at the point of purchase to a household purchasing food with supplemental nutrition assistance program benefits;

children.label: III
children.text: except in the case of projects receiving $100,000 or less over 1 year, would measure the purchase of fruits and vegetables by low-income households participating in the supplemental nutrition assistance program;

children.label: IV
children.text: ensures that the same terms and conditions apply to purchases made by individuals with benefits issued under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 [ et seq.] and incentives provided for in this subsection as apply to purchases made by individuals who are not members of households receiving benefits, such as provided for in section 278.2(b) of title 7, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation);7 U.S.C. 2011

children.label: V
children.text: has adequate plans to collect data for reporting and agrees to provide that information for the report described in subsection (e)(2)(B)(iii); and

children.label: VI
children.text: would share information with the Nutrition Incentive Program Training, Technical Assistance, Evaluation, and Information Centers established under subsection (e).

children.label: i
children.text: maximize the share of funds used for direct incentives to participants;

children.label: ii
children.text: use direct-to-consumer sales marketing;

children.label: iii
children.text: demonstrate a track record of designing and implementing successful nutrition incentive programs that connect low-income consumers and agricultural producers;

children.label: iv
children.text: provide locally or regionally produced fruits and vegetables;

children.label: I
children.text: that provides incentives when fruits or vegetables are purchased using supplemental nutrition assistance program benefits; and

children.label: II
children.text: in which the incentives earned may be used only to purchase fruits or vegetables;

children.label: vi
children.text: have demonstrated the ability to provide services to underserved communities;

children.label: vii
children.text: include coordination with multiple stakeholders, such as farm organizations, nutrition education programs, cooperative extension services, public health departments, health providers, private and public health insurance agencies, cooperative grocers, grocery associations, and community-based and nongovernmental organizations;

children.label: viii
children.text: offer supplemental services in high-need communities, including online ordering, transportation between home and store, and delivery services;

children.label: I
children.text: for extended hours; and

children.label: II
children.text: most or all days of the year; or

children.label: x
children.text: address other criteria as established by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: the improvement of dietary health through increased consumption of fruits and vegetables;

children.label: B
children.text: the reduction of individual and household food insecurity; and

children.label: C
children.text: the reduction in healthcare use and associated costs.

children.heading: Healthcare partners
children.label: 2 Healthcare partners
children.text: In carrying out a project using a grant received under paragraph (1), an eligible entity shall partner with 1 or more healthcare partners.

children.label: I
children.text: prescribe fresh fruits and vegetables to members;

children.label: II
children.text: submit to the Secretary an application containing such information as the Secretary may require, including the information described in subparagraph (B); and

children.label: I
children.text: provide financial or non-financial incentives for members to purchase or procure fresh fruits and vegetables;

children.label: II
children.text: provide educational resources on nutrition to members; and

children.label: III
children.text: establish additional accessible locations for members to procure fresh fruits and vegetables.

children.label: i
children.text: identify the 1 or more healthcare partners with which the eligible entity is partnering under paragraph (2); and

children.label: aa
children.text: screen and verify eligibility for members for participation in a produce prescription project, in accordance with procedures established under subsection (a)(5);

children.label: bb
children.text: implement an effective produce prescription project, including the role of each healthcare partner in implementing the produce prescription project;

children.label: cc
children.text: evaluate members participating in a produce prescription project with respect to the matters described in subparagraphs (A) through (C) of paragraph (1);

children.label: dd
children.text: provide educational opportunities relating to nutrition to members participating in a produce prescription project; and

children.label: ee
children.text: inform members of the availability of the produce prescription project, including locations at which produce prescriptions may be redeemed;

children.label: II
children.text: a description of any additional nonprofit or emergency feeding organizations that shall be involved in the project and the role of each additional nonprofit or emergency feeding organization in implementing and evaluating an effective produce prescription project;

children.label: III
children.text: documentation of a partnership agreement with a relevant State Medicaid agency or other appropriate entity, as determined by the Secretary, to evaluate the effectiveness of the produce prescription project in reducing healthcare use and associated costs;

children.label: IV
children.text: adequate plans to collect data for reporting and agreement to provide that information for the report described in subsection (e)(2)(B)(iii); and

children.label: V
children.text: agreement to share information with the Nutrition Incentive Program Training, Technical Assistance, Evaluation, and Information Centers established under subsection (e).

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: 4 Coordination
children.text: In carrying out the grant program established under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall coordinate with the Secretary of Health and Human Services and the heads of other appropriate Federal agencies that carry out activities relating to healthcare partners.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In carrying out the grant program under paragraph (1), the Secretary may enter into 1 or more memoranda of understanding with a Federal agency, a State, or a private entity to ensure the effective implementation and evaluation of each project.

children.label: i
children.text: a description of a plan to provide educational opportunities relating to nutrition to members participating in produce prescription projects;

children.label: ii
children.text: a description of the role of the Federal agency, State, or private entity, as applicable, in implementing and evaluating an effective produce prescription project; and

children.label: iii
children.text: documentation of a partnership agreement with a relevant State Medicaid agency or other appropriate entity, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The value of any benefit provided to a participant in any activity funded under subsections (b) or (c) shall be treated as supplemental nutrition benefits under section 8(b) of the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ().7 U.S.C. 2017(b)

children.heading: Prohibition on collection of sales taxes
children.label: 2 Prohibition on collection of sales taxes
children.text: Each State shall ensure that no State or local tax is collected on a purchase of food with assistance provided under subsections (b) and (c).

children.heading: No limitation on benefits
children.label: 3 No limitation on benefits
children.text: Grants made available under subsections (b) and (c) shall not be used to carry out any project that limits the use of benefits under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ( et seq.) or any other Federal nutrition law.7 U.S.C. 2011

children.label: A
children.text: be considered part of the supplemental nutrition assistance program benefits of the household; or

children.label: B
children.text: be used in the collection or disposition of claims under section 13 of the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ().7 U.S.C. 2022

children.heading: Nutrition Incentive Program Training, Technical Assistance, Evaluation, and Information Centers
children.label: e Nutrition Incentive Program Training, Technical Assistance, Evaluation, and Information Centers
A
children.text: establish 1 or more Nutrition Incentive Program Training, Technical Assistance, Evaluation, and Information Centers, in consultation with the Director of the National Institute of Food and Agriculture; and

children.label: B
children.text: to the extent practicable, consult on the design and scope of such Centers with grocers, farmers, health professionals, researchers, incentive program managers, and employees of the Department of Agriculture with direct experience with implementation of existing incentive programs or projects.

children.label: i
children.text: collecting and providing information on best practices that may include communications, signage, record-keeping, incentive instruments, development and integration of point of sale systems, and reporting;

children.label: ii
children.text: disseminating information and assisting with collaboration among grantee projects, applicable State agencies, and nutrition education programs;

children.label: iii
children.text: facilitating communication between grantees and the Department of Agriculture and applicable State agencies; and

children.label: iv
children.text: providing support for the development of best practices for produce prescription projects and the sharing of information among eligible entities and healthcare providers that participate in a produce prescription project under subsection (c); and

children.label: v
children.text: other services identified by the Secretary; and

children.label: i
children.text: uses standard metrics with consideration of outcome measures for existing projects;

children.label: ii
children.text: includes to the extent practicable grocers, farmers, health professionals, researchers, incentive program managers, and employees of the Department of Agriculture with direct experience with implementation of existing incentive programs in the design of the instrument through which data will be collected and the mechanism for reporting;

children.label: I
children.text: the results of the project; and

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of grant funds used for the project; and

children.label: iv
children.text: creates and maintains a publicly accessible online site that makes annual reports and incentive program information available in an anonymized format that protects confidential, personal, or other sensitive data.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: To carry out paragraph (1), the Secretary may, on a competitive basis, enter into 1 or more cooperative agreements with 1 or more organizations with expertise in developing outcome-based reporting, at least 1 of which has expertise in the food insecurity nutrition incentive program and at least 1 of which has expertise in produce prescription projects.

children.label: i
children.text: nongovernmental organizations;

children.label: ii
children.text: State cooperative extension services;

children.label: iii
children.text: regional food system centers;

children.label: iv
children.text: Federal, State, or Tribal agencies;

children.label: v
children.text: institutions of higher education (as defined in ); orsection 1001(a) of title 20

children.label: vi
children.text: other appropriate entities as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 1 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $5,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2014 through 2023.

children.label: A
children.text: $35,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2014 and 2015;

children.label: B
children.text: $20,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2016 and 2017;

children.label: C
children.text: $25,000,000 for fiscal year 2018;1So in original. Two subpars. (C) have been enacted.1

children.label: C
children.text: $45,000,000 for fiscal year 2019;1

children.label: D
children.text: $48,000,000 for fiscal year 2020;

children.label: E
children.text: $48,000,000 for fiscal year 2021;

children.label: F
children.text: $53,000,000 for fiscal year 2022; and

children.label: G
children.text: $56,000,000 for fiscal year 2023 and each fiscal year thereafter.

children.label: A
children.text: for each fiscal year the Secretary shall use not more than 10 percent of such funds available for such fiscal year for the produce prescription program described in subsection (c);

children.label: B
children.text: for each fiscal year not more than 8 percent of such funds available for such fiscal year shall be used by the National Institute of Food and Agriculture and the Food and Nutrition Service for administration; and

children.label: i
children.text: $17,000,000 in the aggregate for fiscal years 2019 and 2020; and

children.label: ii
children.text: $7,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 2021 through 2023.
